script: AddText"Entering the station after docking you can see a large emblem of the Nation emblazoned prominently over the main entranceway. Numerous replika units are around working the dockyard with several replikas roaming around with weaponry. Obviously this is a major security zone as most of the patrolling replikas are armed with shotguns, rifles and shields. A stern looking replika security officer steps towards you and to demand that you sign several forms, shoving a rather beaten looking computer pad in your face. After what seems like an eternity you finally sign the last of the forms just for docking. Satisfied, the replika security officer then waves you past the security checkpoint, allowing you to finally begin your errands and business at the station" textGrayColor

AddText"Arriving at Vineta, you are immediately greeted by a large crowd of people waiting for transport to and from the planet. At the security checkpoint the security replika hands you a rather worn looking computer pad. Numerous customs and declaration forms labeled REQUIRED greet your eyes. After a very long time you finally complete the last form and hand the pad back to the replika. Satisfied she opens the security gate and waves you in. As you walk out, you can hear the replika behind you shout 'NEXT' for the next person in line." textGrayColor

AddText"The embarkation area was teeming with a large number of workers and replikas handling large cargo containers being loaded onto trams. Some of the workers and machinery have the telltale signs of tinged with the famous red dusty sands of Kitezh. The local security replika beckons you to sign a form. You give a sigh and head over to begin the long process of paperwork just to pass customs." textGrayColor

AddText"After a very, very long visit at the security booth trying to get the forms signed, ordered and filled out, you exit the docking area to finally enter the main lobby area. The logo of the Nation is exceptionally prominent, a massive golden icon towering over the people walking about. The brillance of color the emblem makes isn't enough to cover up the dreadful rainy gloom outside. The large number of grey apartment building certainly do not help. The sooner ship captains complete their buisness and leave Rotfront, the happier they become." textGrayColor

AddText"From orbit, the yellow clouds obscure Heimat's cities. However once you land, get past the port authority replikas and sign the insane forms, visitors are greeted by a large, almost opulent city. Numerous people walk about with an equal number of replikas of varying shapes and heights doing various tasks. For you and your fellow crewmates, Heimat is probably one of the few places that actually look like people care about appearences." textGrayColor

AddText"Cargo ships, freight trams and workers are a common sight, even at the passenger areas. It seems that despite being a general port, its mostly used for shipping cargo out. Security replikas are much rougher and strict here as the number greeting you don't have the usual security weaponry but much heavier stuff. You can definitely detect an air of fear here. Most of the workers avoid your gaze while the replikas seem to be occupying positions of authority here." textGrayColor

text: You are not generally well known by $faction officials, for good or ill. Like any regular person they just don't care about you.

The $faction authorities tend to view you favorably but not enough to give you much attention. At best, you might be sold some export grade surplus equipment or some low quality consumer goods if you are brave enough or dumb enough to purchase.
Your name is known to some mid-level of $theFaction. The mere mention of your name can cause some officials to break out in a sweat or fidget nervously.

You're relatively well-known within $theFaction, whether in person or by reputation. Low level officials will sometimes try to lavish you with gifts so they can win your favor and hopefully help their careers by putting in a good word.

You know some of the $faction leaders on a personal level. You may receive trade concessions and access to high-quality ships and munitions. Your tales and aid are legendary, and your influence and connections limitless within $faction.

$faction officials tend to view you with suspicion. Access to the port authority is granted only grudgingly, and you find yourself the subject of frequent investigations and harassment by Replika security units.
No shoot on sight orders have been given but @faction forces are on alert and encouraged to make your life miserable.

Your name is known to officials as a menace to society and top enemy of the people. $Faction commanders have authorization to engage your fleet on sight. However, there may still be a way for you to sneak into their markets to do some black-market trading. You might still recover good standing with $theFaction, but it's going to take some serious work or bribery to do so.

A holo of your face and an IDENT-hash of your last known fleet position are both regular parts of security bulletins issued by Protektor forces. It's fairly certain there's a high-ranking $faction officer sent by top levels of the $theFaction whose primary assignment is to hunt you down. For them, you are enemy number 1, a scourge and something to be hated and destroyed.

The bar is crowded with large number of spacers taking a break from their long journey. Of course there are several Replikas but its is hard to discern if they are partaking in the drink or keeping watch over their human counterparts. The bar seems to be selling popular spirits from the sector but thankfully they seem to accept your standard credits as currency. Very few members of the Nation or military members are here. The ones are probably are high ranking officers or top level government officials.
Confirming Volksmarine NIS transponder and encryption code. Good to see you $playerName and have a good day.
Domain-type AIS Code verified. This is $personRank $personName of Eusan Nation. You are in our our self-defense zone and within weapons range. State your intentions, over.
Send word to HQ and request reinforcements! Our forces may be less powerful at the moment but the Nation will prevail!
We are the sword and shield of the Nation! The Volksmarine will not retreat without a fight!
Attention enemies of the Nation. You are outmatched, you are cornered, and you are at our mercy. Surrender now and face justice for your crimes against the Eusan people!

script:
AddText"Hello and thank you for choosing the Eusan Nation. This faction is based off the faction of the same name from Signalis, a horror game created by Barbara Wittmann and Yuri Stern of indie game studio, rose-engine. Many thanks to them for creating a wonderful game that no two people have the same idea of what the story is."

AddText"The Eusan Nation is a totalitarian nation in which in the setting of Starsector, have won their decades long struggle against the Eusan Empire. Now masters of their own star system, they must now contend with the aftermath of the war and the wider Persean Sector. They have been lucky so far with the major powers being rather unconcerned of the struggles of the Eusan people. Now with the system finally under total control of the Nation, the stars are theirs for the taking."

AddText"The Nation is primarily a low-tech faction with lots of missile and ballistic weaponry strapped to rather primitive looking starships. What they lack in technology, they make up for it in soft advantages. Their fleet is rather heavy in frigates, destroyers, and cruisers."

AddText"As this mod is under a work in progress, new features will come online as things progress. I hope you enjoy this mod and thank you for choosing this faction to play."

OR
Welcome back $playerName, what can I do for you?

Hello hello. Got any smokes I can ask of you?

OR

script:
$player.metOfficerYeong = true
ShowPersonVisual
FireAll PopulateOptions
AddText "A series of beeps and high pitched noises your communications request is accepted. The communications terminal begins to show an image of a rather young woman in a military uniform, silver hair and what appears to be a series of adhesive bandages her face."
AddTextSmall "A stressed out military officer of the Nation, Ariane Yeong has spent her formative years living under oppressive umbrella of the Nation. Once she had lofty goals and aspirations as a young girl would have. Now she is tired, haggard, and resigned to a life of working for the Nation's military intelligence services as an analyst." highlight
AddText "Hello this is Officer Ariane Yeong speaking. What do you want?"

text:
YOUR COMMUNICATIONS REQUEST HAS BEEN REJECTED. YOUR ID HAS BEEN LOGGED. 
OR
COMMUNICATIONS TERMINATED. IF THIS HAS BEEN IN ERROR, PLEASE SEE CENTRAL AUTHORITY.
OR
ATTENTION ENEMY OF THE NATION. YOUR ATTEMPT TO BYPASS SECURITY HAS BEEN LOGGED.

options: officeryeongChatPenroseProgramOption:Tell me about the Penrose Program...

text:
"The Penrose Program? If you want to hear about the that its going to be a long story but let me get down to the public facing summary: the Penrose is the Nation's deep space exploration program. That it"

"Given that you have high level access I guess I can tell you about the Penrose Program in detail..."

"The Penrose program in reality is an attempt to show the rest of the galaxy of the power of the Eusan Nation. You seen those posters plastered on the metro lines streets about the Penrose? Its all about telling us, mainly the people of the Nation, how powerful we are."

"We sent out a lot of these craft into the deep void to find planets we can colonize, mine or just expand the Nation. We pair human pilots with a Replika. In this case the LSTR Replikas. The LSTR types are meant for deep space assignments and repair work. The Volksmarine uses a lot of these replikas on their ships. AEON? Depends on the facility."

"The Penrose program has been operating for a long time. I don't have the details available but rumors say that we launched dozens of these ships during the war. How we did that while a major war was happening is beyond me..."

"Anyway that is all I have. Anything else would require a higher security clearance and I don't want to get in trouble by telling you things that isn't cleared with my superiors..."
options: officeryeongChatPenroseProgramOption3: Very well I won't press the matter further...

text:
"Heimat is the captial of our Nation. I assumed you know that by now..." she says. She adjusted her uniform a bit and continued on...

"Heimat is where the magic started. A bunch of miners got fed up, overthrew the Imperial garrison here. They used the leftover materials to expand their military forces and build a proper army. The other planets were already rebelling against the Empire with mixed results. Kitezeh had its rebellion snuffed out relatively quickly. Vineta's fought on for years until the first reinforcements arrived from Heimat."

"Most if not all of the Nation's government agencies resides here on this planet. Volksmarine, Volksarmee, AEON, every branch of government... "

"You know initially the government was supposed to relocate to Vineta after the war but they did a survey of the planet and found out that the planet was just too damaged to consider relocating. Also Heimat was already functioning as the capital of the Nation so it made little sense to relocate to Vineta. Asides from that I guess thats the basics of Heimat."

"Heimat is the home of the Nation's government. That is pretty much it."

"Rotfront...yeah..."

"Rotfront was my home for a good long time. I spent many years of my childhood with my mother cooped up inside a radio station. I had lots of fond memories there. Read a lot of books, did art and pretty much had much of the station to myself. Homeschooled for most of my childhood too.

Her voice trailed off and you can tell there were some really bad memories resurfacing.  

"Then one day my mother told me I was to live with my aunt Kamilla. So I moved from the radio station to the main city at Blockwart C..."

"Went to highschool there. Never liked it. Bullies everywhere." she says, motioning to her hair and eyes as she went on. "Made fun of my hair, my eyes, my family. Made friends of course but that is a story for another time."

"Military service done there at Rotfront, trained and operated as a radio operator. Spent 2 years doing that. Never knew what happened to my friends after high school. Tried getting hold of them but no response to my letters or requests..."

"Anyway, enough chit chat about Rotfront. The less we talk about it, the better..."

"Was born on Leng. I do not have many memories of that place because my mother moved us to Rotfront due to her position as a radio officer."

"As far as I can tell, Leng is the outermost planet of the star system. The Nation's furthest outpost. A shithole from what I gathered. Dodged a bullet right there too. They wanted to send me to Leng after my mandatory military service would you belive that? I applied for a different military service and got accepted...barely from what I was told."

"Leng is not a nice place from what I heard. Unlike Buyan or Rotfront, Leng is literally an airless rock with tunnels for human habitation. You got the orbital and the elevator. Then you have the various rail and tram lines to whatever areas you need to go."

"Also being Leng, the place apparently has a reputation of dread. The rumors of that place housing AEON worksites. All hush hush rumors of course but the rumors are its not a nice place to live, work, or have a family."

"That is all I have about Leng for my clearance level."

options: officeryeongChatSmokingOption: So I noticed you smoke a lot...

text:
"Smoking?" she queries. She gives you a confused look at you then her eyes dart to her lit cigarette and back to you.

"Ohhhh...yeah. Smoking. That...."

"Yeah smoking kills. That is why I smoke. Pretty much my job requires a lot of patience and since I don't have much to do at home or anywhere else, I smoke. I go through a few smokes a day. Yeah yeah lung cancer is a danger. Most cigarette brands sold by the Nation are extremely strong compared to the Imperial brands. What is funny is that Imperial branded cigarettes were prohibited from being sold until fairly recently. Back then anyone caught selling them will get a prison sentence or get sent to a labor camp. "Illegal distribution of banned paraphillia." the law says."

"Owning a packet of Imperial cigs isn't a crime anymore. I can smoke them but since they are so rare these days its almost impossible to get a hold of."

"Interestingly, I hear that the Empire had large stocks of cigs stockpiled for its soldiers and since the war ended, there have been rumors of warehouses being found with massive stocks of cigarettes and other items. There are even rumors that the factories that used to make the cigs are now being retooled to make the same cigs with different packaging. Something more "patriotic" or so they say."

"I just wish someone would give me a pack of the Imperial Era smokes. Old tobacco i heard mellows with age."

"Remember...smoking kills. That is why I smoke."

options: officeryeongNeuralRepStart:"So I have a new task for you if you are up for it"

text:
"The reason why I asked is that you have been deem trustworthy by our security services and even though I don't have much clout, the way things work here I am obligated to conduct a background check for all commissioned officers of the Eusan Nation."

"I have several things on my plate and frankly I don't have enough time to follow up on all of them myself. There is something small and nice for someone like you..." she says, a small, faint smile lighting up. You can see it took some effort for her to smile. You wonder when was the last time she did that.

"Anyway, if you are up to the task, I have an assignment and needs to be done..." she explains...
options: officeryeongNeuralRepBriefing0:"Alright...tell me what needs to be done?"

text:
"The name is Ariane Yeong. You refer to me as Officer Yeong. Remember that next time." she said as she slid a rather thin looking folder with numerous crossed out items and redactions on the front cover.

You open the folder and inside are a series of pictures, a few pages with lists of statistics printed and an old newspaper clipping. You take a look at the pictures and they all seem to show the same building from different angles. Then you look at the newspaper cutout and you seem to notice that it just shows a giant hole on the ground and ruined buildings near it. 

"What your looking at is the Central Neural Archive"
options: officeryeongNeuralRepBriefing1:"Let me guess...someone bombed it right?"

text:
"Yes yes...its a hole in the ground. Nuked by the Empire. So according to my information, the Central Neural Archive was destroyed by an Imperial cruiser several years ago. Probably some remnant Imperials doing retaliatory raids...who cares...the issue here is that I been given an assignment to recover data if there is any from this crater on Vineta" she explained. She puts a finger on the picture as if making a point. An uncomfortable pause occurs. She lifts her finger from the picture and she straightens up.

Ariane stands up and walks over to a file cabinet and pulls out another folder. She opens it and hands you a paper with a picture.

"This man here is our agent sent to recover the data. AEON obviously dislikes certain outside agencies poking their noses into their operations and goes to great effort to discourage it but I have authority from a higher power. Several days ago AEON work crews unearthed a backup data storage from the central computer room. We made requests for the data but AEON refused us at every turn until recently."

"It seems that the political games have soured people in upper management and has forced AEON to cooperate with us. We asked our agent on Vineta to get the data but a traffic control malfunction or so they say has grounded all transport ships. AEON making life difficult for us so we need someone not officially part of our agency to do so. You have some immunity from their nonsense"
options: officeryeongNeuralRepBriefing2:"Right...then what? What is my contact method?"

text:
"Send him a coded message upon arriving at Vineta. Once you set up initial communication, meet with him directly and get the data he is carrying. Fly back to Heimat"

"if we get the info, we can find the spy or narrow down the search. KLBR units will be able to locate the person or persons...and we cripple the spy network."
options: officeryeongNeuralRepBriefing3:"I see. Well thats in it for me?"

text:
"You get paid according to the rules and regulations set forth by the Nation" Ariane dryly responds.

"50,000 credits to be exact."

Ariane stands up from her desk and hands you an envelope. You rip it open and see a list of radio frequencies. 

"Code frequencies and special codewords to contact him. The passphrase is 'I got a package to deliver but the address is in Carcosa.' The response will be, 'I am not sure how I can help but I will try' "

"Alright then. Come back to me when you are ready"
or
"Ok sure. Let me know if you want to reconsider."
"Good. Now the fun begins. For accepting this mission you herby agree to finish the mission and prioritize its completion for the good of the Nation. Do not come back if you have not finished it."

You take a shuttle down to the planet. Flipping through the channels, your comms officer locates the specified frequency and begins to broadcast the coded signal at the specified time. 

After a few seconds of broadcasting, a reply signal comes over. The comms officer quickly decodes the message and hands you the result.

"Recovery Area 5. North Supply depot. Section 11"

You give a nod and set out to the agreed upon location with two of your trusted bodyguards.

The area specified by the coded transmission was near a large crater. The crater used to be a sprawling campus dedicated to the storage of maintenance of neural patterns associated with the creation of Replikas. Now its a hole on the ground, a reminder of the painful sacrifices made by the Eusan Nation in its fight against the empire. 

As you arrive at the meeting spot, a man is busy arranging crates and supplies. He gives you no attention as he busily sorts the supplies.

options: officeryeongNeuralRepAgentTalk1: "I got a package to deliver but the address is in Carcosa"

text:
"I am not sure how I can help but I will try" the man replied

You motion the bodyguards to relax and assume overwatch position. You look at the man and ask him for the information you were sent to get.

"You three characters here for the data right?" the man says rather curtly. He opens his jacket and holds box containing several data disks. Standard issue data storage devices by the looks of it. He hands it over to you which you promptly pocket it.

"AEON knows your here probably but one cannot be too careful. Project manifests, data logs, experimental results, inventory lists its all there." the agent says.

"Now scram before AEON decides to change their minds."

Taking the box of data disks from the agent was easy enough. Although no problems were encountered, there was an sense of unease in the back of your mind. Why would the Nation go through this trouble to get this data? Why would a government agency such as AEON go through all this trouble to erect obstacles for data retrieval? 

You quash the thought. Perhaps all will be revealed in good time. You board your shuttle begin your return trip back to the main spaceport.

options: officeryeongNeuralRepFinal: Update Ariane on the status of your mission.
officeryeongNeuralRepCancel: Tell her that the mission is still ongoing...

text:
"Oh I see..." 

You see a small look of disappointment on her face. You probably should have finished the mission prior to asking her.

"Well then...go and finish it. I will be waiting."

options: officeryeongNeuralRepConfirm: Tell her that you obtained the data from the agent on Vineta.

script:
$global.eusan_nation_neuralrepo_completed = true
Call $global.eusan_nation_neuralrepo_ref updateStage
AddStoryPoints 1
FireAll PopulateOptions
text:
You inform her that you were successful in getting the information from the agent. She seems rather pleased with your success.

"Excellent work. Thank you for helping me out. I wont forget your assistance on this matter. Your job is now complete and your payment should be arriving shortly after you leave this place." she says as she motions you to leave. You feel like being forced out.

As you leave the door you can hear the computer disk drive whirring as it accesses the data. The door closes before you can attempt to guess what may be on the disk.

conditions:
RepGTE "eusan_nation" WELCOMING
$player.commissionFactionId == "eusan_nation"
$id == "eusan_nation_officer_yeong" score: 100
$player.metOfficerYeong == true
$global.eusan_nation_neuralrepo_completed == true
$global.officeryeong_penroserecovery1_start == false
options: officeryeongPenroseRecovery1Start: "A new job came from my superiors about search and recovery..."

text:
As you sit down on the chair in her small office, Ariane takes out a small packet of cigs and lights one up. Taking a deep draw and blows out a sizable cloud of cigarette smoke before she begins to explain your next mission.

"My superiors were pleased you got the data from our agent in Vineta. That wierd chapter is over and whatever issues they had with AEON, its now over. Your new task I have been authorized to give you is more strenuous. More fetch and carry type stuff. There is a whole list of this stuff but I will be giving them to you one by one."

Ariane straightens up and hands you a folder. As you open the folder the 1st page already is filled with redactions. Clearly someone was paranoid about secrets getting out.

"Yes. Its standard procedure honestly. Things are classified even if the information itself isn't really classified." Ariane says. She takes another draw from her cig and blows the smoke towards the celling.

"I assume you know about the Penrose Program. If not, its the Nation's deep space exploration program. Two crew, one human, one replika. Shoved into a deep space scout ship. Shot into deep space at whatever direction the Nation deems worthy of exploring."

"So a few weeks ago the Deep Space Radio Array on Rotfront detected this transmission." Ariane says, pressing a button on a recording device on her desk. It began playing a rapid set of beeps before cutting out and repeating again.

"Listen to that. Its a rather simple transmission. A series of beeps and if you remember your basic codes, beeps and dashes." She stops the recording device with a swift press of a button.
options: officeryeongPenroseRecovery1Briefing1: "So what kind of transmission is it?"

text:
"This is a standard Penrose emergency beacon. Its set to go off when a Penrose craft has suffered severe damage or has crashed landed on a rock. Given how this weak this transmission was received it was a miracle the Rotfront Array picked this up" Ariane says. She then picks up a paper from her desk and began to summarize the information to you in a rather curt manner.

"The transmission is from the PENROSE-419. It was launched over two decades ago. The Flight Officer's name was Cameron Yang. The replika was your standard LSTR unit, LSTR-419. The last known contact was roughly 12 years ago before they dropped out of contact. There was a very brief attempt to restore contact but given 419's mission was essentially over, the Nation did not bother outside the standard hails."

"Given how much time has passed, the mission is one of recovery. The mission recorder is to be recovered, the crew, if found, be given last rites as befitting of..." Ariane squints her eyes a bit at the paper she held.

"Heroes of the Nation" she said with a flat tone.

"Alright. Here is the important part. The transmission was traced to this part of the galaxy. Our analysts have determined that the Penrose is located $eusan_nation_penroseRecovery1_distanceLy light years away in the $eusan_nation_penroseRecovery1_systemName system and the signal seems to be originating from the planet $eusan_nation_penroseRecovery1_planetName"

"As mentioned before, your mission is to recover the mission recorder, any suitable information and if possible...lay the crew to rest."

"Shame. If you want to reconsider, let me know"
OR
"Take your time. Come back later if you want to proceed."
OR
"Ok sure. If you wish to think it through that is fine with me. Come back when you are ready."
"Thank you. Now formally I will need to give you the usual spiel of working for the Nation but I won't. Anyway your mission "

As you enter orbit above the planet, your communication's officer notifies you that a transmission has been detected and on a specific frequency known to be used by the Penrose program. The transmission is a standard emergency beacon, emitting a general distress call and identifier. 

You head over to the communication's officer and begin to examine the data. The transmission was broadcasting on a specific frequency, using a series of dashes and beeps. You note its similarity to the transmission played for you during the mission brief by Officer Yeong. You order your communication's officer to record the transmission and triangulate its location. 

45 mins later the communication's officer finally reports that the location of the Penrose 419.
options:
officeryeongPenroseRecovery1Option1: Send a team down to investigate the crash site.
defaultLeave: Leave

text:
The team's shuttle touched down on the planet with little issue. After a short hike they finally came upon the site where the communication's officer indicated the Penrose-419 would be. The ship seemed to be intact, having appeared to have crash landed. Most of the lower hull was buried and parts of the starboard wing was sheared off. Aside from the damage from the landing and lost wing, there could be no doubt of the identity of the ship with a giant 419 on the intact portside wing.

After a few hours surveying the hull and setting up equipment to enter, the recovery team finally gained entry via the emergency hatch on the top of the hull. The interior of the ship was a charred mess. Debris, burned documents, and wrecked equipment was everywhere.

The team split into two, one to the flight deck, another to the cryo chamber. The team entered the flight deck with no issue but the second team reported that the cryo chamber was wrecked beyond recovery. Entry would be impossible with the equipment they have. No sign of the crew was found but they did find bits of pieces of a Replika unit.

"Sir message from the ground team, they have the mission data recorder and the logbooks." the communication's officer reported.
options: officeryeongPenroseRecovery1Option2: Order your team to return to the fleet.

text:
The team returned to the ship without incident. The recovery team leader presents the container with the mission data recorder and the logbooks.

"Here is the recovered items sir. Its a mess down there and we tried to recover as much as we can. No sign of the flight officer or the replika but we did find parts of one."

You congratulate the recovery team leader for a job well done. Now its time to return to Heimat with the data recorder. Hopefully Officer Yeong will be more forthcoming on what is on the device.

options: officeryeongPenroseRecovery1Final: Update Ariane on the status of your mission.
officeryeongPenroseRecovery1Cancel: Tell her that you have yet to complete the mission.

text:
"So you decided to come and waste my time?"

You can see on her face that she is annoyed by your admission.

"Please let me know if you finished your job."

options: officeryeongPenroseRecovery1ConfirmOption: Tell her that you recovered the mission data recorder.

text:
You hand over the mission data recorder and the logs to Ariane. It takes a few minutes for her to hook up the data recorder to a special machine. At the same time she hands you the logs and tells you to skim over it. Obviously she needs your help so you decide to give it a try.

It takes a few hours of reading of the logs and you can already piece together a story. The logs tell of the Flight Officer, a proud man of Vinetan origin and his mission. As the mission started you can tell his was extremely proud of the Nation and his selection to be part of the Penrose Program. A fine example of a loyal citizen of the Nation.

You continue to read the logs and the story of the proud, patriotic Flight Officer. Around the 2nd Phase of the mission, his Penrose suffers a coolant leak. The leak is repaired but the radiation was enough to leak through a storage compartment and contaminate some food supplies. Although this was not distressing at first, later log entries detail the Flight Officer's growing concern about not having enough food.

Past Phase 3 of the Program, things became more disturbing. Around day cycle 4500 the food supplies became distressingly low. Most Penrose ships had enough food supplies past day cycle 6500 but the radiation leak event cut that down drastically. Around cycle 4881, the officer terminates his Replika and began to eat it.

At this point the log entries began to become more sparse in writing. You turn to Ariane for instructions as the logs by this point become almost useless. She however was busy examining the data from the mission recorder. You tap on her shoulder and she looks up.

"You saw what he did to his Replika right?" she asked. It is almost like she knew what you were about to ask.

"He ate his Replika. That is...not surprising. At least not in the Penrose Program." 

She sits back on her chair and hands you a mission report from another Penrose mission, PENROSE-211. 

"An earlier mission. Most of the early missions were classified but this one I had to ask a lot of favors to get. This flight officer also killed and ate his Replika because he was starving. After Phase 3, most Penrose ships will eventually run out of consumables and supplies to keep the ship operating. Reports of desperate behavior among Penrose flight officers are extremely common"

She points to a page where the final transmission of the PENROSE-211 was received. 

"The officer was a patriot, much like the PENROSE-419's officer. Proud, willing to do anything for the Nation. At the end of his mission, several years after Phase 3, he was busy transmitting insults and hostile messages back to Heimat."

She taps the page as if prompting you to read it. Obviously she cannot say it out loud. 

'Fuck the Nation' one line said.

Another line had: 'You sent me out here to die.' 

'Fuck the Great Revolutionary'

There were several pages worth of transcribed messages. The flight officer was clearly at his wits end. 

"From what I see here from the data recorder from 419, its different in many respects but the behavior was the same as 211. They both felt abandoned and sent to die. Once you hit Phase 3 of the Program, either you found a suitable planet or you failed. 419 failed."

script:
$global.eusan_nation_penroseRecovery1_completed = true
Call $global.eusan_nation_penroseRecovery1_ref updateStage
AddStoryPoints 1
FireAll PopulateOptions
text:
"So ends the story of the PENROSE-419. There are no happy endings for these people"

"You must be wondering why bother recover the data recorders. They all died in service of the Nation right? I also wonder why they bother. The truth of their demise is something I expect the Nation to bury."

"The PENROSE-419 was sent to deep space to search for planets. To expand the Nation, to find new worlds for us to settle. In the eyes of the Nation they did just that. All it cost is one ship, one pilot and one replika. I think my superiors are more interested in how they died rather than why."

Ariane then opens a drawer in her desk and pulls out an envelope. 

"Your reward as promised."

You take the envelope of money from her. You give her a slight nod and exit out her office.

options:
officeryeongChatOptions: "Tell me about..."
officeryeongMissionOptions: "Do you have any work for me?"
officeryeongChatOptionsEscape: "I got to go now"

text:
"Sure. What do you want to discuss?"
OR
"I can spare some time to talk a bit. What do you want to talk about?"
OR
"Ask away."
OR
"What do you want to know?"

"Hmmm...let me check if there is anything you can help with."
OR
"Perhaps. Let me see if I have anything outstanding at the moment."
OR
"Help? Let's check the list shall we..."

"Talk to you later then..."
OR
"See you next time..."
OR
"Come back later. I may have other stuff that you can help with."

"Mmmhm...let me know if you want to know more stuff..."
OR
"Talk to you later then..."
OR
"Remember, whatever we speak here, probably should not be said anywhere else. Just a precaution"
OR
"See you next time..."

script:
SetEnabled officeryeongComplementResult false
SetTooltip officeryeongComplementResult "You recently done this. Wait for a few days."

SetEnabled officeryeongInsultResult false
SetTooltip officeryeongInsultResult "You recently done this. Wait for a few days."

text:
"Flattery is nice"
OR
"Thank you but your not my type"
OR
"Why thank you for the nice words. You just made my day slightly brighter than normal"
OR
"Do you mean it?"

"That is not nice..."
OR
"Aye go away then..."
OR
"Uh huh...right then..."
OR
"Insulting me right here right now? Wow..."

script:
SetEnabled officeryeongGiftOptions false
SetTooltip officeryeongGiftOptions "You recently done this. Wait for a few days."
options: officeryeongGiftOptions: Give Officer Yeong a Gift
officeryeongGiftLobsterOffer: Offer Officer Yeong a lobster from Volturn

text:
"Thanks for the gift of lobster. Now I don't have to eat the ration packs they give us." $personLastName says. "Do you know how many time I have eaten the same ration pack this last week?"

$personLastName takes the gift from you. She seems slightly pleased with the fact she isn't going to eat the same dish this evening. 
OR
"Oh? A lobster? The last time I had lobster was when i was a child. My mother managed to get lobster from Vineta but even then it was a bit old."

You hand her the lobster. She gives you a small nod in appreciation.
OR
"Oh my lobster? How did you get this? Did you swipe it from a shipment or bought it from a market? In fact don't tell me I don't want to know..."

She takes the lobster off your hands. You swore you can see a faint smile as she takes the gift.

options: officeryeongGiftLobsterAsk:Ask Officer Yeong how she feels about Volturn Lobsters

text:
"Lobsters? I do not have time to get anything fancy for food, not that I can get them anyway. I only eaten lobster once and it was for a special occasion. That was years ago."

"Wait...what do you mean blue lobsters?"
OR
"Lobsters...from Volturn? I have heard of them but I never seen one in person. I assume they have claws and stuff..."

"I bet it costs a lot just to ship them here"
OR
"I am not sure. Volturn is far away and I don't have time to fly around or travel. Even if I had the ability, I would need to pass checkpoint after checkpoint just to leave this planet. Not worth the time and effort just to sample a dish."

"Maybe one day..."
OR
"Honestly, the rumor is that lobsters are shipped here as food for the upper echelons of the Nation's government here. I never seen any type of seafood here, Volturn or from Vineta."

"Why you ask? Are you trying to get me one?"

options: officeryeongGiftLobsterPlushOffer: Offer Officer Yeong a soft plush lobster toy

text:
"Thanks for the gift." $personLastName says.  "It be hard to keep this gift because contraband isn't allowed and i am pretty sure this is contraband."

She looks happy but as she moves away you can see her edging towards the trash bin...
OR
"What a pitiful looking mascot. I love it." as $personLastName takes the plush lobster from you. "Thanks for the gift but please don't give me things like this in the future i can get in trouble..."

She gives the plush toy a small pat and disappears into her office. You hear a clunk of a trash bin lid.
OR
"The laws of the Nation are pretty strict. I can't recieve gifts or they will be considered bribes." $personLastName says. "Forturnatly I have the means of keeping gifts or sanitizing them. Thanks anyway."

She gives you a faint nod and she walks away with the plush. You swore you could have heard the disposal chute turn on...

options: officeryeongGiftLobsterPlushAsk:Ask Officer Yeong how she feels about a stuffed toy lobster

text: "test"

options: officeryeongGiftChocoLavaOffer: Offer Officer Yeong a chocolate lava cake

text:
"Huh? Interesting I never seen this type of dessert before" $personLastName says. "This is actually pretty good. Where does it come from?"

She pokes the cake a bit...chocolate filling begins to ooze out.

"In fact don't tell me. Its better if I don't know". 
OR
"Oh my what is this thing? A cake? And its filled with a sweet gooey filling? How decadent...Nothing like this comes to my desk" $personLastName says. You can tell she is exceptionally fascinated by the cake you gave her.

She gives the cake a small nibble. Chocolate filing oozes out and she gives a small smile.

"Thanks...this means a lot to me"
OR
"You really should not be giving me gifts its considered illegal and against the laws I could get in trouble...is that cake? With a sweet filling?" $personLastName says. She moves her hand to poke the filling that oozed out. She gives a small lick and her eyes brighten.

"This is really good. Thanks for the gift!"

options: officeryeongGiftChocoLavaAsk:Ask Officer Yeong about chocolate lava cakes

text: "test"

"Wait you were going to give me something?"
OR
"Perhaps it be best if you did not"
OR
"Whatever..."
